report_name: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Flaw That Allowed Service Principal Takeover

vulnerabilities.name: Microsoft Entra ID Agent ID Administrator Role Privilege Escalation
vulnerabilities.description: A design flaw in the 'Agent ID Administrator' role in Microsoft Entra ID provided overly broad permissions, allowing a user with this role to add themselves as an owner to any service principal. This enabled credential injection and privilege escalation, potentially to Global Administrator.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of Entra ID who patched the design flaw in its 'Agent ID Administrator' role in April 2026.

identities.name: Silverfort
identities.description: Security research firm that discovered and responsibly disclosed the Entra ID design flaw.

identities.name: Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC)
identities.description: Microsoft's security team that received the responsible disclosure of the vulnerability from Silverfort.

indicators.name: Entra ID Service Principal Ownership Change
indicators.pattern:
title: Entra ID Service Principal Ownership Change
id: 2b1c8e9f-5d7a-4b3c-8a1f-9e2d7c6b5a4d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects when a new owner is added to a service principal in Microsoft Entra ID. This is a high-privilege operation and should be monitored, especially for changes to critical service principals. This was the method described as a potential exploit path for the 'Agent ID Administrator' role flaw patched in April 2026.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/microsoft-patched-entra-id-role-flaw.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1098
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Add owner to service principal'
    Result: 'success'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the addition of a new owner to a service principal in Microsoft Entra ID audit logs, a behavior that could indicate abuse of overly permissive roles like the formerly vulnerable 'Agent ID Administrator' role.
